rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Ex Parte Application For Order Directing Deposit Of Referee Fees Or, In The Alternative, For Order Amending Reference

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Modifying The Pro Rata Shares Of The Cost Of The Discovery Referee Based On Petitioners Economic Hardship
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Respondent has not carried his burden of showing that he has an economic inablility to pay a pro rata share of the discovery master's fees. The information Respondent has provided to the Court shows that he has significant wealth. Even assuming he has spent the substantial income he has received over the past years, he continues to hold substantial real and personal property assets. His choice to hold his wealth in these forms, while perhaps not the wisest portfolio management strategy under the circumstances, cannot be a basis to conclude that he cannot bear his fair share of litigation expenses. He has the economic ability to do so. Therefore the Court will not modify its prior order in this regard. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise opposing parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, January 24, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Declaration Of Carlos Mcclatchy Regarding Economic Inability To Pay For Discovery Referee Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: The motion to seal is denied. The declaration does not include the kinds of private information normally sealed, such as bank account numbers or details, or tax information. It includes no specifics concerning his real or personal property holdings, some of which is available through public records. It includes only general statements intended to support Petitioner's claim of indigency that he has made repeatedly in the open record. Petitioner has put his financial standing at issue, and his personal desire to shield the general information in this declaration does not outweigh the public's interest in free access to the courts. In any event, the requested sealing is not narrowly tailored to address only whatever specific, private information it is that Petitioner feels should be excluded from public access. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise opposing parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, January 24, 2017.
